# VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0006 - QUIC RFC 9000 DPLPMTUD Probe Tracking Verification

## Scope

This verification covers the bounded RFC 9000 Section 14.3 DPLPMTUD helper subset: PMTU probe packet-number tracking, acknowledgment/loss outcomes, padding-based probe sizing, BASE_PLPMTU initialization, and per-address-pair MPS isolation. It excludes automatic probe scheduling, packet-builder integration, OS ICMP ingress, alternative-path search, and public API claims.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0001
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0002
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0003
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0004

## Verification Method

Execution and inspection of the helper-backed DPLPMTUD probe-tracking state, requirement-home tests, fuzz coverage, and BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic solution builds successfully.
- The RFC 9000 S14P3 requirement-home tests are discoverable by the test runner.
- The benchmark project can execute a filtered BenchmarkDotNet Dry run.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the S14P3 requirement-home tests for positive, negative, edge, and fuzz coverage.
- Run the DPLPMTUD BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job to validate the permanent benchmark harness.
- Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
- Confirm that no broader PMTU scheduler, packet-builder, socket-ingress, or public-support claim is introduced.

## Expected Result

The helper starts each path at QUIC v1's 1200-byte BASE_PLPMTU, tracks only probes larger than the current per-path MPS, promotes MPS only on acknowledgment of the matching address-pair probe, records loss without promotion, computes nonzero PADDING bytes for padding probes with ack-eliciting payload, and keeps path outcomes isolated.

## Evidence

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicDplpmtudState.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0001.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0002.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0003.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S14P3-0004.cs
- benchmarks/QuicDplpmtudStateBenchmarks.cs
- artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260422-2343-dplpmtud-dry/
- dotnet test Incursa.Quic.slnx --filter "FullyQualifiedName~REQ_QUIC_RFC9000_S14P3"
- dotnet run -c Release --project benchmarks/Incursa.Quic.Benchmarks.csproj -- --job Dry --filter "*QuicDplpmtudStateBenchmarks*"

## Status

Passed locally for the focused S14P3 requirement-home coverage and the new `QuicDplpmtudStateBenchmarks` Dry suite. The proof remains limited to the helper-backed DPLPMTUD subset and does not claim probe scheduling, packet-builder integration, OS ICMP ingress, alternative-path search, or public API support.

## Related Artifacts

- SPEC-QUIC-RFC9000
- ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0006
- WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0006

## Benchmark Scope

The permanent `QuicDplpmtudStateBenchmarks` suite covers padding-probe acknowledgment, padding-probe loss, and independent per-address-pair probe tracking. The 2026-04-22 Dry run is preserved under `artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260422-2343-dplpmtud-dry/`; Dry warnings are limited to very small iteration times and should not be interpreted as stable performance numbers.
